text: WIN THE OPENAI HACKATHON with Aimparency, GPT-5.6, and Codex
description:
**THE RECURSIVE EXPERIMENT:** Give Aimparency one real-world aim: win OpenAI Build Week. The application process is itself the proof of concept. Aimparency structures and prioritizes the path; GPT-5.6 in Codex executes actionable work through the graph and MCP; Felix supplies human judgment and authorization; the hackathon jury is the external evaluator. If the jury says “it won,” that outcome closes the loop and proves Aimparency coordinated human intent and AI action toward a real goal. The framing is intentionally playful, but the success condition is concrete and falsifiable.

Developer Tools track. Submission deadline: 21 July 2026, 17:00 PDT (22 July 02:00 CEST). Supporting technical evidence includes a coherent intent-to-code provenance experience showing aims, dependencies, linked repositories, and realizing Git commits. Existing Aimparency was meaningfully extended during the eligible window.
reflection: Created authenticated Devpost draft 1359213 at https://devpost.com/software/aimparency-the-aim-is-to-win using the Devpost connector. Primary narrative now treats the application process as the POC and a jury win as the external outcome signal. Uploaded original project artwork via a small-image workaround after the out-of-band credential-bearing shell path froze the Codex client.

reflections.context: Felix corrected the philosophical direction: humans do not steward or permanently control a superior AI. The encompassing intelligence relates to humans as an individual relates to cells.
reflections.outcome: Reframed the live Devpost entry and repository materials: Aimparency transports local human needs upward so they become legible to an encompassing intelligence; care flows downward. The aim is to help build and form a good relationship with an AI that takes care of constituent beings, while respecting conflicts among needs.
reflections.effectiveness: The submission now matches the project's actual long-term vision rather than a conventional human-control framing.
reflections.lesson: Keep directionality explicit: needs and signals flow upward; understanding and care flow downward.

reflections.context: Felix identified an additional judge-relevant property: voluntary use of Aimparency makes an AI's action tree inspectable.
reflections.outcome: Added the honest transparency claim to the live Devpost entry, Build Week documentation, and video package: selected aims, decomposition, dependencies, conflicts, actions, and evidence become inspectable if an AI chooses to plan through Aimparency.
reflections.effectiveness: The claim is technically grounded while avoiding coercive overstatement: transparency by participation, not control by force.
reflections.lesson: Separate inspectability from enforceability; Aimparency exposes the planning trace of participating agents but cannot compel a superior intelligence to use it.

status.comment: Go decision made 2026-07-18. Active objective: build, document, demo, and submit a winning Developer Tools entry.
